senses.definition: related to or using a temperature scale on which water boils at 212° and freezes at 32°
of or relating to the usage of a scale for measuring temperature in which water freezes at 0 degree and boils at 100 degrees
related to or using a temperature scale on which water boils at 100° and freezes at 0°
a set of twelve items

examples.x: She bought a dozen roses to surprise her partner on their anniversary.
The restaurant offered a special deal: buy a dozen doughnuts and get one free.
The farmer harvested a dozen eggs from the chicken coop every morning.
He ordered a dozen pens to stock up for the upcoming school year.

senses.definition: a unit for measuring weight equal to approximately 28.34 grams
a unit used to measure liquids in the United States, equivalent to approximately 3.785 liters
a unit of length equal to one-twelfth of a foot or 2.54 centimeters
the size, amount, or degree of one thing compared with another

examples.x: The scale of the earthquake was so immense that it caused widespread damage across the region.
When choosing a new car, he considered the scale of fuel efficiency in relation to the vehicle's size.
The scale of the forest fire was beyond anything the firefighters had ever encountered.
The artist had a remarkable ability to capture the scale of natural landscapes in his paintings.
We need to assess the scale of the problem before deciding on a suitable solution.

senses.definition: an individual part used to build or create something

examples.x: Each piece of the puzzle was crucial in completing the picture.
The furniture came in several pieces, requiring assembly before use.
The engineer examined each intricate piece of the machine to ensure it was functioning correctly.
The mechanic replaced the broken pieces of the engine, restoring the car to working order.

senses.definition: a small cut of a larger portion such as a piece of cake, pizza, etc.

examples.x: She took a slice of bread and spread some butter on it.
He ordered a large pepperoni pizza and ate three slices by himself.
She cut the cake into eight equal slices and gave one to each of her guests.
He enjoyed a slice of cheese with his wine.
She sliced the apple and gave him a slice to taste.

senses.definition: bread that has a particular shape and is baked in one piece, usually sliced before being served

examples.x: The loaf was sliced into even pieces for breakfast.
He placed the warm loaf on the cooling rack.
A whole loaf was baked for the family gathering.
She bought a fresh loaf of bread from the bakery.
Can you pass me the loaf from the bread basket?

senses.definition: an amount of a substance in the shape of a block such as chocolate, soap, etc.
something that is wrapped around a tube or shaped into one

examples.x: She handed me a roll of film to develop at the photography store.
He carried a roll of cash in his pocket for emergencies.
The photographer loaded a fresh roll of film into the camera before the shoot.

senses.definition: the quantity of things or people

examples.x: The number of attendees at the event far exceeded our expectations.
Can you please double-check the number of chairs we have in the conference room?
The number of accidents on this road has increased significantly over the past year.
We need to reduce the number of errors in our reports to improve efficiency.
The number of books in the library's collection continues to grow each year.

senses.definition: a group of things sharing the same quality, usually connected to each other

examples.x: She picked a bunch of flowers from the garden for the centerpiece.
He carried a bunch of keys, each opening a different door in the building.
The children gathered a bunch of leaves to make a nature collage.
The grocer tied a bunch of bananas together for display.
She found a bunch of old letters tied with a ribbon in the attic.

senses.definition: a noticeably huge number or amount of a particular thing

examples.x: The team had a pile of work to finish before the deadline.
After the storm, there was a pile of debris scattered across the neighborhood.
As the event ended, there was a pile of leftover food that needed to be donated.

senses.definition: a group of people or objects placed in a line

examples.x: She carefully arranged the books in a straight row on the shelf, organizing them by genre.
At the movie theater, we chose seats in the middle row for the best view of the screen.
The farmer planted the seeds in a neat row, ensuring each one was spaced evenly apart.
During the game, the fans cheered enthusiastically from the front row, eager to support their team.

senses.definition: the outer part of an area or object that is furthest from the center

examples.x: He stood on the edge of the cliff, admiring the breathtaking view below.
The knife slipped off the edge of the cutting board and fell to the floor.
The cat balanced precariously on the edge of the windowsill.
The ice skaters glided gracefully along the edge of the frozen lake.
She ran her finger along the edge of the book's pages, feeling the texture of the paper.

senses.definition: any object that can be used to store something in, such as a bottle, box, etc.

examples.x: She placed the leftover food in a container and put it in the fridge.
He bought a decorative container for storing his jewelry.
The kitchen was filled with various containers for spices and herbs.
The plastic container kept the cookies fresh for days.
She filled the container with water.

senses.definition: a box made of cardboard or plastic for storing goods, especially liquid

examples.x: She poured milk from the carton into a glass.
The carton of juice was stored in the refrigerator.
She picked up a carton of yogurt from the shelf.
The carton was labeled with the expiration date.
The carton was sealed tightly to prevent leaks.

senses.definition: a container in which goods can be stored and safely carried around

examples.x: She packed her clothes into a large case for the trip.
The case was locked to keep the valuables secure.
The case was made of leather, giving it a stylish and professional look.
I lost my case on the way to the airport and had to report it.
She put her makeup in a small case to take to the wedding.

senses.definition: a paper or cardboard container that is used to store items of the same type within, such as cigarettes
a small bag typically made of paper, plastic, etc., that can contain various things, such as tea, sugar, or spices

examples.x: I used a packet of sauce to flavor the pasta.
They gave us free ketchup packets with our meal.
The tea came in small, sealed packets.
I bought a packet of seeds for the garden.
She stored the remaining spices in a resealable packet.

senses.definition: a box or container in which items are packed

examples.x: She received a package in the mail containing her new shoes.
The fragile items were carefully wrapped and placed in a sturdy package.
He sent a package to his friend overseas for their birthday.
The delivery driver dropped off the package at the front door.
The package was labeled with instructions to handle with care.

senses.definition: a large cup which is typically used for drinking hot beverages like coffee, tea, or hot chocolate

examples.x: She sipped her morning coffee from a favorite ceramic mug adorned with colorful flowers.
The cozy café offered a selection of mugs for customers to choose from, each with its own unique design.
He cherished his old, chipped mug, a relic from his college days that held sentimental value.
The souvenir shop sold mugs featuring famous landmarks from around the world, appealing to tourists.
She handed me a mug of tea as we sat by the fire.

senses.definition: a deep round container with a handle and a very narrow opening that is used for keeping liquids, usually has a stopper or cap

examples.x: With a smile, the bartender filled our jug with frothy beer, signaling the start of a festive evening.

senses.definition: a container with a wide opening and a lid, typically made of glass or ceramic, used to store food such as honey, jam, pickles, etc.

examples.x: She carefully filled the jar with homemade strawberry jam, sealing it tightly with a lid.
The kitchen shelves were lined with colorful jars filled with spices, grains, and dried herbs.
He picked up the pickle jar from the pantry, intending to enjoy a tangy snack.
The candlelight flickered softly, casting shadows on the rows of jars that adorned the shelves.
With a gentle twist, she opened the honey jar, savoring its golden sweetness as it flowed onto her toast.

senses.definition: a flexible container that is used to store thick liquids

examples.x: He squeezed the toothpaste from the tube.
They used a metal tube to measure the liquid accurately.
She inserted the paper into the tube and sealed it for mailing.
The artist dipped his brush into the paint tube.
The lifeguard blew the whistle through the plastic tube.

senses.definition: a flat object with elevated edges, often used for holding or carrying food and drink

examples.x: She served the tea on a silver tray.
The waiter carried a tray of drinks to the table.
The tray was full of colorful fruits.
The hospital food was served on a plastic tray.
He used a tray to carry his breakfast upstairs.

senses.definition: a container, made of metal, used for storing food or drink

examples.x: The vending machine was stocked with various cans of fruit juices and iced teas.
She opened a can of soup for a quick and easy lunch.
I opened the can of soda and had it with my sandwich.

senses.definition: an object, usually made of wicker or plastic, with a handle for carrying or keeping things

examples.x: She filled the basket with fresh fruits and vegetables from the local farmers' market.
He carried a picnic basket to the park, eager to enjoy a meal outdoors with friends.
They placed a basket of snacks on the table for guests to enjoy during the party.
The children used a basket to collect Easter eggs during the annual egg hunt.

senses.definition: a round open container, made of plastic or metal, with a handle used for keeping or carrying things

examples.x: The painter dipped his brush into the bucket of paint to start working on the walls.
We used a large bucket to collect apples during the harvest in the orchard.
The old barn had a leaky roof, so they placed buckets underneath to catch the rainwater.
The fisherman returned from the lake with a bucket full of freshly caught trout.
After the storm, they used a bucket to bail out water that had collected in the basement.

senses.definition: more than enough or the amount needed

examples.x: She packed extra clothes in case of unexpected weather changes.
There was extra food left over after the party, enough to last for days.
He brought extra cash to cover any unplanned expenses.
The teacher provided extra materials for students who wanted to delve deeper into the topic.

senses.definition: the highest amount, degree, or extent there is or is possible, allowed or needed

examples.x: The hotel offers a maximum of two complimentary meals per day.
The airline allows a maximum of one carry-on bag per passenger.
The conference room can accommodate a maximum of 50 attendees.
All employees are entitled to a maximum of ten vacation days per year.

senses.definition: the lowest amount, degree, or extent there is or is possible, allowed or required

examples.x: We need to keep disruptions to a minimum during the construction process.
Each student is required to attend a minimum of three lectures per week.
During the pandemic, it's crucial to keep social interactions to a minimum to reduce the spread of the virus.
The tour package guarantees a minimum of five tourist attractions to visit.
To maintain a healthy work-life balance, try to keep overtime hours to a minimum.
The job requires a minimum of five years of experience in the industry.

senses.definition: very little in quantity or amount

examples.x: Our budget for the project is limited, so we need to be mindful of our expenses.
The restaurant has limited seating capacity, so it's best to make a reservation ahead of time.
The store had a limited supply of the new video game, so it sold out quickly.
The limited number of seats at the concert made tickets highly sought after.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something has increased twice in number, amount, or extent
used to indicate an amount equal to one part of two equal parts of something

examples.x: She spent half an hour reading before bed.
Half of the cake was already eaten by the time I arrived.
The team scored half as many points as they did in the previous game.
Half the class decided to participate in the science fair.

senses.definition: used to show that something is as much or as many as it is needed or wanted

examples.x: Do you have enough money to cover all your expenses this month?
We need to buy enough supplies to last us through the winter.
Make sure you drink enough water during your workout to stay hydrated.
Enough had attended the meeting to make it official.

senses.definition: goods in a small bag that is typically made of paper, plastic, etc.

examples.x: The kids ate a packet of candies, to satisfy their sweet tooth.
The hotel provided a complimentary packet of toiletries for each guest.

senses.definition: a small amount, quantity, or piece of something

examples.x: He took a tiny bit of cake from the platter.

senses.definition: the quantity or the contents within a deep large container with a wide mouth and a handle

examples.x: The campers filled their canteens from a pitcher of fresh spring water.
For brunch, they enjoyed mimosas made with a pitcher of freshly squeezed orange juice and champagne.

senses.definition: a deep round container with a handle and a curved opening, used for pouring liquids

examples.x: The pitcher was filled to the brim with ice-cold lemonade, perfect for a hot summer day.
She carefully poured the tea from the pitcher into each guest's cup, making sure not to spill a drop.
The pitcher slipped from his grasp, crashing to the floor and shattering into pieces.
After the game, the team gathered around the water cooler to refill their pitchers and hydrate.
Grandma's old pitcher, passed down through generations, held sentimental value beyond its practical use.
